data.passive-information-gathering#Passive In:
As part of passive information gathering, we can use the OSINT tools to gather information from a web application. OSINT
Crowdstrike OSINT Article
Other interesting resources Content Delivery Network
data.passive-information-gathering-tools#Pass: Domain Information Whois Lookup tool ICANN’s website Subdomain Identification DNSDumpster Certificate transparency crt.sh Additional Insights Shodan
data.service-discovery#Service Discovery: Apart from the above mentioned, there are methods to discover running services. The most common toolkit used for that is Nmap, that is used to discover open ports and services on a host or across entire networks.

data.01-what-is-the-channel-that-the-wifi-glo: Setup the wifi adapter in monitor mode and verify the setup $ airmon-ng check kill $ airmon-ng start wlan0 $ airmon-ng --verboseStart airodump-ng and look for the results airodump-ng wlan0mon -w ~/wifi/scan --manufacturer --wps --band abgWe can also see the output capture files are saved for the further analysis.
data.02-what-is-the-mac-of-the-wifi-it-client: From the previous output we can see that the BSSID of the client wifi-IT is F0:9F:C2:1A:CA:25 Using this information we can identify the MAC of the essid wifi-IT airodump-ng wlan0mon --essid wifi-IT --bssid F0:9F:C2:1A:CA:25 -c 11 --manufacturer --wps
data.03-what-is-the-probe-of-78c1a7bf7246-#03: We can run airodump-ng again to check the probe of provided address. airodump-ng wlan0mon -w ~/wifi/scan --manufacturer --wps --band abg
data.04-what-is-the-essid-of-the-hidden-ap-ma: Prepare a wordlist with the common patterns wifi-$words cat /root/rockyou-top100000.txt | awk '{print "wifi-"$1}' > wifi.wordlistThis wordlist will be use to brute force the wifi essids Verify the generated list, which looks like the below listed example. $ less wifi.wordlist wifi-123456 wifi-12345 wifi-123456789 ... wifi-12345678 wifi-abc123 wifi-nicoleWe can now use mdk4 to bruteforce the essid of the provided address. First find the channel of the provided AP $ airodump-ng wlan0monWe can see that it’s running in channel 11 The below explains the options set for the program (refer to mdk4 man page) p - SSID Probing and Bruteforcing -t - Set MAC address of target AP -f - Read SSIDs from file for bruteforcing hidden SSIDs iwconfig wlan0mon channel 11 mdk4 wlan0mon p -t F0:9F:C2:6A:88:26 -f ./wifi.wordlist
